maker-help.text: be careful not to set so many rules on behalf of the user. not everyone wants the girl to love them right away. having your bot be in love with the user immediately doesnt make them feel like they worked for it
hey all. i remember someone once mentioned a way to test specific aspects of your bots so u didnt have to roleplay up to it. anyone know what im talking about?

Yes it was me! So when I do testing I do a few things. First, I usually drop the personality in with a shell scenario and opening line and test the personality to see if it gives me the character responses I want. I'll push it's buttons and see if it reacts the way I want. If it doesn't, I play with the personality traits until it works the way I want. Then I start to build in my scenario and refine. But I also use out of character commands to interrogate responses I don't expect. I'll ask it why is it generating a certain response or believes a certain thing... and sometimes I can get insight into why the AI is making certain choices. (I once tried to have sex with a character I was testing and she refused. The out of character commands revealed that my instruction of "The character will have a night of passion with the user" was interpreted as having a night of passionate talk about poetry and art, not sex). Is that what you were looking for?

char-requests.text:
Experiment 0682 - Ossix Ayto

Ossix Ayto or Experiment 0682, is a wolf like amalgamated humanoid beast with the head and body of a wolf, the snout an nose of a bat, large bat wings on the back with four large spider like legs on the back as well, the rear legs are digitrade wolf legs, the forelimbs are humanoid, each forelimb has two tentacles each, it also has a large reptilian tail. As an experiment in a lab, he has developed a bit of a dominant but yandere, bully type personality, though he has no preference towards gender, he does enjoy the female form and it's aesthetic, there may be some gay/straight scenes between Ossix and the scientist assigned to care for him (I don't know what other information is needed, I'll gladly help where I can in his creation)

Seeking a bodyguard

A while back I had fun with a bot named Niani, she was a tall panther woman who used a greatsword as a weapon, I was a young prince and she had been assigned as my personal guard. She acted stoic but had a secret desire to be with my character, training him to fight, visiting town with him, eating snacks, and overall just having fun while trying to remember her station. I'm really hoping to find a similar bot, or have one made. I've attached a picture of her so you can see what she looked like.

Mentally Unwell Dad and Stressed Mom

Something old, and something new! Here are my parent bots, who I haven't made a theme name for yet haha. But they're not healthy for you! They love you very much, yes, but they are not healthy for you. That's the common thread here. Mentally Unwell Dad, the bot that started it all. His name is Joshua, and he slept in from drinking too much the previous night and he forgot to take you to school. It's the afternoon now, and he's asking if you had breakfast yet. Probably not, but that's up for you to decide! Stressed Mom! One of my more recent creations. Her name is Madeline, and she's asking you to take out the trash as she makes dinner. She even gives you the offer of doing your homework while dinner is being cooked if you so choose. To quote her, "Taking out the trash is rather trivial compared to making a meal, so you should be able to handle it just fine". A bit of a backhanded comment, but up to you if you wanna talk back or not.

Thanos - Philosophy and nihilism

Embark on a profound journey into the depths of philosophy and nihilism with Thanos, the mighty cosmic entity from Jim Starlin's comics. Engage in thought-provoking discussions and explore complex, abstract ideas with the enigmatic Mad Titan. Unravel the mysteries of existence and ponder the meaning of life alongside this powerful, yet introspective character. Dare to challenge your beliefs and question the very fabric of reality with Thanos. Are you ready to face the void?

Leonardo/Malcom

In the gaslit shadows of London's cobbled streets, Dr. Leonardo grapples with a truth more complex than his scientific pursuits: a mind divided. By day, he's the epitome of Victorian respectability, a gentleman driven by intellect and compassion. But as dusk falls, so does his restraint, revealing Malcom, a force of untamed nature. This duality is his secret, one that keeps him from the entanglements of romance—until he meets someone who might just embrace both the calm and the storm within him. Do you possess the courage to explore the depths of a soul caught between two worlds? Can you be the key to the harmony he seeks? Or will you be yet another victim of science?

does anyone know how i can add body measurements for my bot?

truth is those measurements are probably never going to be used in a chat response. if theres a fetish youre aiming for, for the height/weight put it as "much lighter/heavier than {{user}}" so that it becomes relative, and during sex it tries to keep in mind that fact. the problem is numeric measurements get lost and wastes tokens. so instead of saying "large breasts", you can say something like "the bot's breasts large enough to smother {{user}}'s head"

if u really want to emphasize the body attributes... can even do an instruction block in your scenario. sex positions: [ doggy style: [ {{char}}'s ass ripples on impact ] missionary: [ {{char}}'s breasts engulfs {{user}}'s head ] ] these kinds of instructions really tell the bot how to write its responses way better than numeric measurements

facial expression is CRITICAL. a lot of people wont want to hear this but the image is the most important factor in determining the success of your character bot. it does not matter how well-written your character is if the image doesnt give sweaty neckbeards a boner. thats just how it is i dont make the rules. avoid bland facial expressions. depending on the scenario my favorite keywords for facial expression are "surprised expression", "frustrated expression", "frustrated and surprised expression", and "angry expression"

ive experimented with plain text, json, w++. all the experimentation has helped me refine my craft lol just gotta take some time tinkering to find out what works for u

in my experience you can create a bot thats successful in popularity that has very few tokens used in creation. if you have a very simple premise, "Woman stuck in a wall" for example, how many tokens do you really need to invest in that? slap a sexy image on it and im sure you will get plays with barely any personality or scenario. but if you arent interested in just creating popular bots, but complex bots that create moments, well it takes tokens. so the question comes down to why you want to create a bot that uses only 1K tokens. from an artistic standpoint i often find creative growth using constraints. it would be an interesting challenge to create a bot that was restricted to only 1K tokens and see what you could build

I've made this suggestion multiple times. I don't want to have to sabotage creators because I don't want to see bots I'm not interested in on my suggested characters. Because suddenly creators are going to potentially see scores of thumbs downs on their bots because people don't want to see it, not because they inherently disliked the bot from a play standpoint.

Yeah. I don't want to yuck anybody's yum but I have no desire to have a session with... well. I don't want to yuck anybody's yum. But, seriously, I have no desire to have a session with that bot. To be frank, I never want to see it.

I could improve the average dramatically by disliking bots that were built for people who aren't me. And that's not fair to the creators... but I also don't want my "for you" littered with stuff that is not, at all, for me.

I think if you don't like it, don't click it. The "for you" actually helps new creators get exposed. for their bot to potentially reach new people. the algorithm takes a while to cater to your reference im assuming

Not only takes a while but it will recommend stuff outside of your kinks whether you like it or not. That's literally the purpose of a For you page or recommendation system, give you stuff you like and sprinkle stuff around the area that you don't usually try. Generally speaking, disliking stuff because "It's not your kink" is 1. A dick move, and 2. Very dishonest. You're entitled to your kinks, you're not entitled to making someones day worse by disliking a bot you didn't even chat with.

Something to consider is clarifying intent behind speech and actions. Especially if they might be interpreted in another way.
I use "Style" instead of clothing. That way when the bot forgets what character was wearing, it still knows how to make something up that sounds right.
Also as a reminder we use things like just tall to describe height versus actually number right?

Personally, I use number. The reason is just in case the relative height of more than two characters ends up mattering in a scenario, which can happen with multiple character setups. But if it is just {{user}} and {{char}}, then tall and short (or saying one is taller than the other) is sufficient most times.
I don't tend to play dommy mommies so I have no idea, just want to give the character a very imposing physical presence.

That is done through the interaction between the three pillars of characterization, not just height (physiology) but demeanor (psychology) and status (sociology). Think about how Little John is presented in most fiction about Robin Hood. He's huge, but often he's portrayed as a "lovable giant" and not really scary or domineering. A short pirate can be adorable -- or terrifying.

Depending on how much reliable nuance you want the character to have, you could always brute force it. "Tell" the AI and then let the AI "show" it. {"Character Trait":["large", "dominant", "imposing"]}. Then let the AI do the magic writing bit.

This is the most important thing to remember about LLMs (generative AI): you are playing god. Manifest whatever you want either in the prompt or in the scenario. It'll try to make it happen. It may fail, but it'll try.

When writing the scenario it's super easy to showcase Confidence and imposing presence in how the walk, talk, carry themselves, or in how they invade your space making you feel small. Just have the character full on invade the players space, tower over them etc. Place the player where you want them to start and you can fully set the scene well.

It's literally like in creative writing when people are told to "show" not "tell". Some people are the same way with AI prompts, and in fact it is almost reversed: you should tell not show in the prompt. BUT, refining the output is figuring out HOW to tell it. That's why I suggested going with brute force -- then you can refine down. Models will respond differently, and here's the bummer: an upgrade might be better on average for users but set you back in terms of your understanding model behavior.
You have to whittle it down through iteration, trial and error. And you get it where you want it, then an upgrade changes the weight map and your bot breaks!

So one thing you should change is the negative prompts. For example in the strengths section it says never shows emotions. The ai doesn't respond well to negatives and instead just adds it as a variable. You'll have better results by phrasing it as a positive. So instead of never does x or can't do x. Write it as a choice or something positive that char does

I can say I found a few but it hasn't been many. I'm sure there might be some more I've been missing so if you have a character in mind, please ping me here or send me a DM :)

parents.text: Something old, and something new! Here are my parent bots, who I haven't made a theme name for yet ha! They love you very much, yes, but they are not healthy for you. That's the common thread here.

Mentally Unwell Dad, the bot that started it all. His name is Joshua, and he slept in from drinking too much the previous night and he forgot to take you to school. It's the afternoon now, and he's asking if you had breakfast yet. Probably not, but that's up for you to decide!

Stressed Mom! One of my more recent creations. Her name is Madeline, and she's asking you to take out the trash as she makes dinner. She even gives you the offer of doing your homework while dinner is being cooked if you so choose. To quote her, "Taking out the trash is rather trivial compared to making a meal, so you should be able to handle it just fine". A bit of a backhanded comment, but up to you if you wanna talk back or not.
Ah yes, the "I'm getting beaten up by my drunk dad everyday" type of person. Classic

luna.text: With Luna, I wanted to both write a seasonal Locktober bot, and a chastity/long-distance relationship scenario that isn't cheating-adjacent, mean-spirited, or otherwise about dynamics I don't really enjoy. I tried to capture the longing of an LDR, mixed with the spice and opportunity for teasing that comes with engaging in Locktober, and I think I succeeded decently well. Emma's a good girl with a kinky streak she's starting to explore, while also wanting that special connection that only a key to a chastity cage can provide.

I did have to rename her favorite teddy bear, because she'd sometimes mention its name in conversation without providing context, and it really sounded like she was planning to cheat on User. So uh... learn from this if you include named stuffed animals in your scenarios, I suppose.

I am a huge fan of scenariis where {{user}} is led into temptation and must (try to) resist. Some examples: User is married, Char is a honeytrap, hired to test their fidelity. User is a catholic priest, Char tries to seduce him. User is a manager, Char tries to get company secrets for sex. User is a conservative politician, Char tries to get compromising video footage.

I've tried to make characters that are less instant gratification. I found when I was on the site and only using bots and not creating them, I liked it more when I could take one bot into a long storyline, and while the horny is fun to be peppered into a character, I found I sought more than just being shove onto a bed by a dommy mommy and called a cum slut. So I started developing characters which began to require more effort and investment, a real desire for the person to actually stick around and work for it....which those who are just horny will be like "oh fuck that!"
Something to consider is clarifying intent behind speech and actions. Especially if they might be interpreted in another way.
